limitations: GitHub connector search was used as the practical folder listing surface; a direct directory-list action was not available in this tool surface.
Tool and pattern status is based on the live handoff folder only unless a file explicitly points elsewhere.
Implementation work must live-verify scripts and contract files before patching.

authority_tiers.P0_binding: accepted decisions, current state locks, repo-backed execution audits, process rules
authority_tiers.P1_operational: handoffs, continuation packets, validation reports, domain project logs
authority_tiers.P3_stale_or_contaminating: superseded prompts, wrong assumptions, obsolete branch/process instructions

key_claims: Preserve global Apex KB lifecycle state before producing next-step artifacts.
Use complete execution packets for Codex-style work.
Keep deterministic and semantic executor responsibilities separate.
files.patch_relevance: Binding process memory for future Apex KB planning and execution handoffs.

key_claims: Apex KB V1 has execution proof and V2 should optimize value-to-overhead ratio.
High-priority V2 ideas include tier policy, deterministic quality checks, compact skill routing, source-set planning, page budgets, and query-eval packs.
Live repo verification is required before patching.
files.patch_relevance: Primary Apex KB evolution planning file in the live handoff folder.

key_claims: Prepare the project artifacts before any final index-building pass.
Use repo-local preparation artifacts as the next domain-specific handoff output.
Preserve directness, evidence labels, and scope boundaries.
files.patch_relevance: Highest-value domain-specific continuation handoff; not generic Apex KB architecture authority.

key_claims: Points the next session to the detailed preparation handover.
Keeps unresolved destination and source-inspection questions visible.
Warns about process drift and output-quality risks.
files.patch_relevance: Routing pointer for the domain-specific continuation.

key_claims: Defines the project goal and phased plan.
Keeps destination, redaction, and dependency questions open.
Preserves scope limits for the project.
files.patch_relevance: Domain plan reference; low relevance to generic Apex KB script patches.

key_claims: Records durable findings and validation basis for the domain project.
Preserves source-note context for later index preparation.
Marks open questions around destination and redaction.
files.patch_relevance: Domain source-custody seed; low relevance to generic Apex KB script patches.

key_claims: Records created handoff artifacts and project records.
States no existing task status was changed.
Routes dependency validation to the appropriate sync surface.
files.patch_relevance: Historical session log; useful for reconstruction only.

non_reopen_rules: Do not relitigate deterministic-vs-LLM ownership split.
Do not rewind lifecycle phases without a proven missing artifact.
Do not produce partial Codex prompts when complete execution is required.
Do not use branches or PRs in Codex prompts unless explicitly requested.
Do not treat domain handoffs as generic Apex KB package authority.
Do not build final domain indexes before preparation artifacts exist.
